codex_response:
Implemented the `58743b76` ARC2 package in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_58743b76/__init__.py#L1), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_58743b76/verifier.py#L1), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_58743b76/generator.py#L1). The rule is: find the corner `2x2` color key, normalize the puzzle to a canonical orientation, then recolor the sparse work-area marker color by the four equal quadrants using the corresponding key cell.

I used the summary hint as a starting hypothesis and kept it because it matched the official examples. No `notes.md` was needed. The generator stays on-family by explicitly building the `8`-colored L-frame, a distinct `2x2` key, a single sparse target color scattered across all four quadrants, and then varying the corner by symmetry. No new shared primitive was added.

Validation passed end to end: official verifier `3/3`, build produced `1000` examples, generated-example verification passed `1000/1000`, and I visually checked [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/58743b76/previews/preview_01.png).
